book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.name: Dark Ages Manual
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.tooltip: A survival guide for the Dark Ages of Ascension of Ages.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.welcome.name: Welcome
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.welcome.descri: What the Dark Ages are and why they feel the way they do.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.welcome.what_a: You are weak, exposed, and intentionally resource-poor.

The Dark Ages are the opening era of Ascension of Ages. You spawn with nothing, into a world that actively pressures you through temperature, thirst, hunger, hostile nights, and seasonal weather. That is by design. The pack is built so your first campfire, first shelter, and first clean water source feel like real progress. Do not rush. Stabilize before you explore.

The Dark Ages teach shelter, food, water, temperature management, and primitive tool progression. You will work with wood, stone, and eventually copper. Advanced materials like iron, diamonds, and digital storage are intentionally unavailable. Other dimensions are locked. The chapters that follow walk you through the survival systems and the forging path step by step. If something feels restricted, it is by design.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.theme.name: Theme and Design
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.theme.descript: Why the Dark Ages are gated the way they are, and how locks behave.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.theme.why_lock: Restrictions are progression features, not bugs.

Ascension of Ages uses a stage-based progression system. Items, recipes, dimensions, and even some mob spawns are gated behind stages that you earn by completing quest milestones. In the Dark Ages, most advanced content is locked. You cannot craft iron tools through normal means, you cannot enter the Nether, and you cannot use digital storage. These locks exist because the pack wants each age to feel distinct and earned.

Locked items are hidden from recipe viewers and cannot be used, equipped, or placed. You can pick them up and store them, but they will not function until you reach the required stage. If you find a locked item in a chest, hold onto it; it may become usable in a later age. The progression system tracks your stages individually, and each stage unlocks the next tier of content. Follow the quests to advance.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mods.name: Mods of the Dark Ages
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mods.descripti: The signature mods that define the Dark Ages experience.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mods.cold_swea: Body temperature as a survival mechanic.

Cold Sweat adds a body temperature stat that responds to biome, time of day, weather, armor, and heat source proximity. You can freeze in cold biomes and overheat in hot ones. Management tools include insulating armor like Goat Fur and Chameleon Molt, the Waterskin for portable temperature drinks, the Boiler for shelter heating, and the Icebox for cooling. See the Mechanics chapter for countermeasures and the matching FTB quest chapters for the items themselves.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mods.thirst_wa: Hydration as a separate survival bar.

Thirst Was Taken adds a hydration bar that depletes over time and faster in hot biomes or summer. Drinking unpurified water can make you sick. Pure water comes from heat-treated containers: smelt a Terracotta Bowl of water in a Furnace to purify it. The mod is purely about water and does not change food or temperature. Pair Thirst Was Taken planning with Cold Sweat to survive cross-biome travel.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mods.ecliptic_: A four-season climate cycle with crop and weather effects.

Ecliptic Seasons cycles the world through Spring, Summer, Autumn, and Winter. Each season changes ambient temperature, crop growth rates, and weather patterns. Some crops will not grow in Winter at all. The mod ships tools to track and manage the cycle: a Calendar for current season, a Thermometer for temperature, a Hygrometer for moisture, and Greenhouse cores for crop shelter. Farmer's Delight and Cold Sweat both interact with the seasonal state.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mods.farmers_d: Deeper cooking, food prep, and farming utilities.

Farmer's Delight expands vanilla cooking with cutting boards, cooking pots, skillets, and a wide library of food recipes. You will use the Cooking Pot for stews and complex meals, a Knife on the Cutting Board for raw prep, and the Skillet for fast pan-cooked meals. The mod also adds Rich Soil for higher-yield farming. In the Dark Ages it is your primary path to meal variety and nutrition above bread-and-water.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mods.aquacultu: Expanded fishing as a parallel food track.

Aquaculture replaces vanilla fishing with a deeper system: dozens of fish species per biome, several rod tiers with different catch rates, the Stone Fillet Knife for faster fish-to-fillet conversion, the Tackle Box, and bait. Fishing is a season-independent protein source that keeps working when Ecliptic Seasons winter kills your crops. Cook fillets in Farmer's Delight equipment for variety.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mods.overgeare: The forging substrate. Replaces the normal tool path.

Overgeared is the pack's canonical path for tools and armor. It replaces vanilla crafting for tools entirely: no ingot-on-grid recipes, no normal smithing template upgrades. Instead, you knap stone, smelt copper, forge at a Smithing Anvil, and gate every tier behind a Blueprint. This mod carries forward through every later age. See the Recipes chapter for the deep mechanic explanation and the step-by-step Entering the Iron Era walkthrough.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mechanics.name: Survival Mechanics
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mechanics.desc: Temperature, hydration, seasons, food, and shelter mechanics that define early survival.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mechanics.temp: Cold Sweat and Thirst create real environmental pressure.

Cold Sweat adds body temperature as a survival mechanic. You can freeze in cold biomes or overheat in hot ones. Craft a Boiler to warm your shelter, an Icebox to cool it, and a Waterskin to carry temperature-regulating drinks. Insulating armor made from Goat Fur or Chameleon Molt helps you survive outside. The quest chapter Stone, Water, Weather and Wounds walks you through each of these items. Prioritize a Waterskin and a Boiler early.

Thirst Was Taken adds a hydration bar. Drinking unpurified water can make you sick. Craft a Terracotta Bowl early for a basic water container. Heat purifies water, so smelting water containers is the early solution. Secure clean water before long trips. Always carry backup water, especially in hot biomes or during summer.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mechanics.seas: Ecliptic Seasons and early food create pressure that rewards planning.

Ecliptic Seasons cycles the world through Spring, Summer, Autumn, and Winter. Crops grow at different rates by season, and some may not grow at all in Winter. Craft a Calendar to track the current season and a Thermometer to monitor temperature. The Greenhouse Core Container and seasonal greenhouse cores shelter growing areas from seasonal penalties. A Hygrometer helps monitor moisture. The quests reward these tools as you hit farming milestones.

Early food is intentionally manual and slow. Start with a Stone Hoe and plant Wheat Seeds. The quests reward Potato and Carrot seeds as you progress. Bread is your first reliable staple. A Smoker speeds up cooking. Fishing provides an alternative protein source, and a Stone Fillet Knife improves fish processing. Keep backup road food, cook better meals at home, and start storing ingredients. Variety matters for nutrition; one food type is not a sustainable plan.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mechanics.shel: Sleep, shelter, and why early exploration is dangerous.

A Bed sets your spawn point and lets you skip the night. The quest line rewards a Sleeping Bag for portable rest while exploring. Later, Rope and Nail plus a Hammock give you daytime-to-night control for tasks that need darkness. A Campfire provides warmth and light but remember that smoke in enclosed spaces can harm you. Build a roof, light a fire outside or with ventilation, and always have a way to sleep before dark. A Crafting Table on a Stick gives you portable crafting for emergencies.

Nights in the Dark Ages are genuinely dangerous. Without armor, weapons, and a safe camp, you are likely to die. Leather Armor is your first real protection, requiring leather from hunting. Do not wander at night without preparation. Torches mark safe paths and prevent spawns. Early exploration should be a loop: leave camp, gather, return before dark. As you build up insulating armor and better tools, you can extend your range. The pack rewards caution, not recklessness.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mechanics.wate: Camp setup, temperature, hydration, and basic protection.

This chapter teaches the core survival loop. It starts with a Campfire quest to confirm you are ready, then guides you through crafting a Furnace, Bed, Chests, and Torches. From there it branches into Cold Sweat items: a Boiler for shelter heating, Waterskin for portable temperature drinks, and an Icebox for cooling. Thirst is addressed through the Terracotta Bowl. The chapter also covers insulating armor from Goat Fur and Chameleon Molt, and introduces Comforts items like Sleeping Bags and Hammocks.

Do not skip the Waterskin quest. Temperature management becomes critical during seasonal extremes and biome travel. Craft the Boiler before winter arrives. Collect Goat Fur and Chameleon Molt when you find the animals rather than waiting until you need the armor. The Leather Armor quest needs the Waterskin branch and the leather-gathering step before it. Purified water from heat-treated containers is safer than raw water. The portable Crafting Table on a Stick unlocks after chests and lets you craft on the move.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mechanics.food: Agriculture, seasons, fishing, and food variety.

This chapter builds your food infrastructure. It starts with a Stone Hoe and Wheat Seeds, progresses through growing wheat and potatoes, then teaches bread baking, composting, fishing, and smoking food. The Ecliptic Seasons branch introduces the Calendar, Thermometer, Hygrometer, Greenhouse Core Container, Growth Detector, and Seasonal Prayer Scroll. A Blackwood Sapling quest offers an alternative wood source. The chapter rewards seed variety and seasonal tools to reduce farming pressure over time.

Plant crops early. Wheat is slow but reliable. Potatoes scale well once you have enough. Do not rely on one food source. The Calendar quest rewards you with a Calendar and Bone Meal, so complete it as soon as possible. Build a Greenhouse before winter to keep crops growing year-round. The Fishing Rod provides protein that does not depend on seasons. A Stone Fillet Knife from Aquaculture improves fish yields. The Smoker is a direct upgrade over furnace-cooking for food. Plan your farm before you need it, not after you are starving.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.recipes.name: Recipes and Forging
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.recipes.descri: Recipe changes, the Overgeared forging deep-dive, and the iron-era progression spine.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.recipes.recipe: What has been changed and why.

Several mods add their own copper tools and armor that would bypass the Overgeared forging system. These recipes have been removed: AllTheOres copper ore hammer, Create copper diving gear, and Create Stuff 'N Additions copper tools and armor. If you see a copper item in JEI but cannot find a crafting recipe, the Overgeared forging path is the intended route. This ensures copper progression goes through knapping, smelting, and smithing rather than simple grid crafting.

Apotheosis normally adds smithing upgrades that let you convert stone tools to iron, iron to gold, and gold to diamond using smithing templates. All of these tier-skip recipes have been removed. The intended progression is stone through Overgeared knapping, then copper through Overgeared forging, then iron through the Medieval Times Overgeared chain. There are no shortcuts. Loot tables are also adjusted: gem drops like diamonds and emeralds are restricted from chests until you reach later stages. Coal, charcoal, flint, and quartz are available in early loot.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.recipes.overge: The forging system in depth: knapping, anvils, the minigame, and blueprints.

Overgeared replaces the vanilla tool flow. You cannot put ingots in a 3x3 crafting grid and get tools. Every tool above wooden tier passes through a multi-stage workflow: knap stone into rocks, shape those rocks into tool heads, smelt copper, forge tool heads on a Smithing Anvil. The reward is that every forged item carries a Forging Quality (Poorly, Well, Expertly, Perfectly, Masterwork) that affects durability, attack damage, attack speed, and mining speed. This is not a vanilla extension. It is a full replacement. There are no shortcuts in this pack.

The chain starts with Flint. Right-click stone with Flint to knock loose Knappable Rocks (Flint can break, so bring spares). Hold a rock and right-click to open the knapping interface, a 3x3 grid where you shape stone tool heads by clicking the correct cells. Assemble shaped heads with sticks for stone tools. The first smithing hammer is the Copper Smithing Hammer, crafted from copper ingots and sticks (knapping yields a stone hammer head, but there is no wieldable stone hammer). Sneak + right-click a stone block with it to create a Stone Smithing Anvil. The Stone Anvil works for early forging but is capped at Well Forged quality and has lower durability than the full Smithing Anvil.
The Smithing Anvil and the Minigame

The full Smithing Anvil is created by holding a Smithing Hammer (Copper or, later, Steel) and sneak + right-clicking a vanilla Anvil. Sneak + right-click the Smithing Anvil to open the forging UI. The UI has a 3x3 ingredient grid (tool head, handle, modifiers) and a hit-timing minigame: swing the hammer in rhythm to land good hits. Quality tiers, worst to best: Poorly Forged, Well Forged, Expertly Forged, Perfectly Forged, Masterwork. Quality affects durability, attack damage, attack speed, and mining speed across the board. Wooden Tongs (early) or Iron and Steel Tongs (later) protect you from hot-metal damage during the forging process.

Blueprints set the ceiling on forging quality. The Drafting Table turns Empty Blueprints into finished ones. Tiers are Poor, Well, Expert, Perfect, and Master. Any tier satisfies the Dark Ages capstone. Higher tiers unlock higher forging ceilings as the blueprint gains uses. Duplicates start at a lower tier. Tool heads can be Polished or Unpolished on a Grindstone. Polishing raises forging quality but is optional. Overgeared tool casts are a separate track. Fire and smelt tool casts only in a vanilla Furnace or Overgeared's Casting Furnace. Modded furnaces such as Iron Furnaces strip a fired cast's shape and hand back a blank Clay Tool Cast, so keep casts out of them.

Mekanism Tools weapons, tools, and armor are forged on the Smithing Anvil, not the crafting grid. Their old grid recipes have been removed. Lay the material ingots into the anvil's forging pattern and strike the metal with a hammer until the piece is finished. This covers every material set: bronze, steel, lapis lazuli, osmium, refined glowstone, and refined obsidian. Paxels are still assembled in the grid from their forged axe, pickaxe, and shovel, and shields are still built in the grid from the forged material. Each Mekanism gear item unlocks with its age, so nothing here lets you skip ahead.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.recipes.materi: What you can use now and what is locked until later.

In the Dark Ages you have access to wood, stone, and copper. Copper is the highest tier metal available and must be processed through Overgeared forging. Basic storage uses vanilla Chests. Basic lighting uses Torches. Basic cooking uses Furnaces, Campfires, and Smokers. Non-Overgeared copper gear recipes from other mods have been removed to keep the forging path canonical. If a copper tool recipe does not appear in JEI, it was intentionally removed in favor of the Overgeared version.

Iron tools require completing the Overgeared forging chain and reaching Medieval Times. Diamonds, enchanting tables, beacons, and advanced ore materials like nickel, silver, and platinum are locked until much later stages. Digital storage systems, advanced machinery, and other dimensions are all gated behind future ages. AllTheOres provides the generic ore system for the pack. When ores become available in later stages, they follow AllTheOres conventions. Do not worry about locked content now. Focus on mastering copper-tier survival.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.recipes.enteri: The Overgeared tool progression from flint to blueprint.

This chapter is the tool progression spine of the Dark Ages. It starts with gathering Flint, then using it on stone to collect Knappable Rocks, shaping a Stone Pickaxe Head via knapping, assembling a Stone Pickaxe, mining Copper Ore, smelting Copper Ingots, and crafting a Copper Smithing Hammer. The hammer lets you convert stone into a Stone Smithing Anvil and forge copper tool heads like Sword Blades and Pickaxe Heads. From there you craft Wooden Tongs, a Grindstone, a Drafting Table, Empty Blueprints, and finally a completed Blueprint.

Do not try to skip steps. The Overgeared chain is linear and each step depends on the previous one. The Copper Smithing Hammer requires copper ingots, which require smelting raw copper, which requires a stone pickaxe. Knappable Rocks come from using flint on stone, and the flint can break, so bring spares. If you cannot find the knapping recipe, check JEI for Overgeared entries. The final quest turns in a drafted Overgeared blueprint of any quality and grants you the Medieval Times stage. This is the capstone.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.loot.name: Structures and Loot
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.loot.descripti: Why structure loot is gated and what to expect.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.loot.loot_gati: Structure loot is intentionally restricted by stage.

Structure chests and mob drops are filtered by your current progression stage. In the Dark Ages, you will not find diamonds, emeralds, advanced alloys, or machine components in loot. This prevents early exploration from bypassing the forging and quest progression. Coal, charcoal, flint, quartz, and basic supplies can still appear. If you find an item you cannot use, it may be stage-locked. Hold onto it in a chest. It may become usable after you advance to a later age.

Structures are worth exploring even in the Dark Ages. Early loot includes food, basic materials, and quest-relevant supplies. But do not rush large dungeons unprepared. Bring torches, a shield when available, food, purified water, and a sleeping bag for longer trips. Mark your path. The pack rewards careful exploration loops: leave camp, scout a target, loot what you can, and return. Some structures contain locked treasure that will become much more valuable in later ages. Patience pays off.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.troubleshootin: Answers to common questions and confusion points.
Why things are locked and what to do about it.

Items are locked because you have not reached the stage that unlocks them. The pack uses a progression system where each age grants access to new tiers of content. If an item tooltip says it requires a specific stage, you need to complete the quest milestones for that stage first. Raw diamonds unlock in Medieval Times; diamond gear, diamond ore blocks, and diamond-tier tools unlock in Renaissance. Iron tools require Medieval Times. Digital storage requires much later stages. Check your quest book for the next milestone. If an item is hidden from JEI entirely, it is locked at a stage you have not reached yet.

Why can I not enter the Nether? All other dimensions are locked until the Renaissance stage. Focus on the Overworld for now. Why can I not craft iron tools normally? Iron tools use Overgeared forging, not grid crafting. Complete the Entering the Iron Era chapter first. Why can I not use this material? Check which stage it requires. Most advanced materials unlock in later ages. What should I do next? Open your quest book. The three Dark Ages chapters show your active objectives. If all quests in a chapter are complete, move to the next chapter. The capstone is in Entering the Iron Era.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.capstone.name: Leaving the Dark Ages
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.capstone.descr: How to complete the Dark Ages and earn Medieval Times.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.capstone.leavi: Complete the blueprint chain to earn Medieval Times.

To leave the Dark Ages, complete the Overgeared chain in Entering the Iron Era and turn in a drafted Overgeared blueprint at the capstone. Blueprint tiers are Poor, Well, Expert, Perfect, and Master. Drafting rolls quality at random. Any tier satisfies the quest. Higher tiers raise forging ceilings later but are not required for Medieval Times. The capstone grants the stage automatically when the quest completes.

Medieval Times unlocks iron-tier tools through Overgeared, early machinery footholds, better utility items, and the first dependable logistics. New quest chapters become available. The survival pressure eases as your equipment improves, but the pack continues to gate content behind stage milestones. Everything you learned in the Dark Ages, temperature management, hydration discipline, food variety, and the forging workflow, carries forward. The Dark Ages were the foundation. Medieval Times is where you build on it.

book.aoa.medieval_codex.welcome.where_yo: Iron unlocked, kinetic power available, dimensions still locked.

Iron is unlocked. Raw diamonds drop now, but diamond gear, diamond ore blocks, and diamond-tier tools stay locked until Renaissance. All other dimensions, Nether, End, and Aether among them, stay locked until Renaissance. Create's kinetic blocks are usable: water wheels, windmills, gearboxes, the mill, the press, the mixer. Overgeared continues. There is no iron smithing hammer; the tier after the Copper Smithing Hammer is the steel one (the plain Smithing Hammer, forged from a steel hammer head). Forge iron at the full Smithing Anvil, made by sneak-right-clicking a vanilla anvil with a Smithing Hammer. Temperature, thirst, and seasons stay active.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.mechanics.kineti: Create runs on stress. Generators (water wheels, windmills, hand cranks) produce capacity. Machines (mill, press, mixer) consume it. If consumption exceeds capacity, the network stops. Water wheels need flowing water against at least one face. The First Mill chapter walks the build.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.mechanics.forgin: Iron tools come from the Smithing Anvil, not grid crafting. Heat the ingot, forge a head, assemble onto a handle. There is no iron smithing hammer; the tier after the Copper Smithing Hammer is the steel one: the plain Smithing Hammer, forged from a steel hammer head. To move past the stone anvil's quality cap, sneak-right-click a vanilla anvil with a Smithing Hammer to build the full Smithing Anvil. Every forge rolls quality from Poorly through Masterwork, affecting durability and damage. Reforge at the Grindstone if the roll comes out poor. If you use tool casts, fire and smelt them only in a vanilla Furnace or Overgeared's Casting Furnace. Modded furnaces such as Iron Furnaces strip a fired cast's shape and return a blank Clay Tool Cast.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.mechanics.surviv: Cold Sweat, Thirst Was Taken, Ecliptic Seasons, and Farmer's Delight all continue at Medieval. Winter still kills crops. Heat still drains thirst. The survival loop is unchanged. Keep your Boiler, Icebox, and Waterskin available.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.recipes.core_wor: Mechanical Press, Millstone, and Basin come through the First Mill chapter. Add the Mechanical Mixer from Metallurgy when you need fluid recipes. One water wheel can feed all four in a starter layout. The basin sits under the press or mixer. You keep this kit through Renaissance and into IR.
Iron tier unlocked, diamond tier still gated.

Open in loot: iron tools, iron armor, raw iron, basic Create components, basic Overgeared materials, and raw diamonds. Closed: diamond gear, diamond ore blocks, and diamond-tier vanilla tools, which unlock at Renaissance. Closed: every modded mid-tier material like Mekanism alloys and IE plates, which unlock at IR and later. Keep locked items in a chest. They become usable at the appropriate stage.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.capstone.leaving: Three great hunts and the Renaissance unlock.

The Medieval capstone is the What Waits in the Grove chapter. Fell at least three of the four great beasts: Ferrous Wroughtnaut, Foliaath, Frostmaw, and Grottol. Then complete Master of the Grove. That completion unlocks Renaissance: six more dimensions (Nether, End, Aether, Eternal Starlight, Undergarden, Otherside), the vanilla enchanting table, diamond tier, and the age's magic mods. Magic Foundations closes on the Neo Vitae Athanor with Malum, Theurgy, and Occultism required. Spectrum, Mahou Tsukai, and Forbidden Arcanus open as further Renaissance depth. See the Bosses category for each beast's location.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.bosses.name: Bosses
book.aoa.medieval_codex.bosses.descripti: Where to find the great beasts of the Medieval hunt.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.bosses.naga.name: Naga
book.aoa.medieval_codex.bosses.naga.page: From Mowzie's Mobs. The Naga spawns naturally in the Overworld on high stony beaches and mountainous shores (beachy mountains, beachy hills, and stony shores), at Y 68 and above under open sky at night. It is stage-locked to Medieval Times: before Medieval, your attacks pass through it. Hunt it after the Medieval unlock.

A fast, venomous serpent miniboss. Keep moving and punish its recovery; ranged weapons help. It drops Naga Fangs, which craft the Naga Fang Dagger (poisons targets, deals double damage from behind) and poison Darts for the Blowgun.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.bosses.ferrous_w: Mowzie's Mobs: iron juggernaut of the Wrought Chamber.

From Mowzie's Mobs. The Ferrous Wroughtnaut waits inside underground Wrought Chambers, roughly Y 20 to 50, in any non-ocean Overworld biome. The chambers are fairly common; search caves and mined tunnels for a small stone room with an armored figure inside. It is stage-locked to Medieval Times: it cannot be harmed before Medieval.

It blocks every frontal attack with its armor and shows a boss bar. Circle behind it and strike its back; bait its overhead smash to get past. It heals when out of combat and resets if you die nearby, so commit to the fight. Drops the Wrought Helm and the Axe of a Thousand Metals.
From Mowzie's Mobs. Foliaaths spawn naturally in Overworld jungles, on leaves, logs, or the ground at Y 60 and above, at night. They are not stage-locked, so you may meet one before Medieval. Bring armor if you hunt it early.

A predatory plant that lunges when you step close. Bait the bite, retreat out of range, then punish the cooldown. It drops Foliaath Seeds: plant one on grass and feed it meat to grow a tame Foliaath of your own.
From Mowzie's Mobs. Frostmaws generate as a surface structure in snowy Overworld biomes (not forest, taiga, ocean, river, or beach), roughly Y 50 to 100, and avoid villages. You will see a frost-bound figure resting on the surface. Not stage-locked.

It sleeps guarding an Ice Crystal. You can sneak up and steal the crystal with the right method to weaken it, or wake it and fight. It has a boss bar, heals while asleep, and resets if you respawn nearby. Drops the Ice Crystal, which channels an icy whirlwind.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.bosses.grottol.d: Mowzie's Mobs: living ore deep underground.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.bosses.grottol.p: From Mowzie's Mobs. Grottols spawn deep underground in the Overworld, at Y 16 and below, in darkness with no sky view, on stone in any non-mushroom biome. Not stage-locked, but rare. Mine deep to find one.

A living mineral creature: it must be mined with a pickaxe, not swords. A Fortune pickaxe yields more ore; Silk Touch captures it as a Captured Grottol. Blowing it up or smelting it also work. Drops ore and the Captured Grottol item.
Six dimensions, Magic Foundations enrollment, enchanting, diamond tier.

Six dimensions unlock: Nether, End, Aether, Eternal Starlight, Undergarden, Otherside. Magic Foundations enrolls you through Neo Vitae's Athanor, with required Malum, Theurgy, and Occultism spines feeding that close. Spectrum has its own Renaissance chapter. Forbidden Arcanus and Mahou Tsukai open as age depth outside Foundations. The vanilla enchanting table works. Diamond tier is usable. The Renaissance runs ten required quest chapters plus optional depth, so pace yourself; this is the densest age in the pack.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.mechanic: Magic Foundations Enrollment
Three required schools converge on the Neo Vitae Athanor.
Foundations

Magic Foundations closes on the Neo Vitae Athanor and grants ren_magic_foundations_complete. Malum, Theurgy, and Occultism are required school spines in that chapter. Each lane must reach its spine end before the Athanor proof unlocks.

Neo Vitae is the altar path that becomes the Athanor. The other three schools feed into that close. Spectrum lives in Spectrum Academy, not Foundations. Forbidden Arcanus is not a Foundations school. Deeper Occultism Djinni work, Theurgy Digestion Vat, and F&A Aureal Tank unlock at Industrial.
Scriptura Vitae
Neo Vitae is the required magic spine: Renaissance through Gilded.
The Blood Pact

Neo Vitae is the pack's required magic spine, threading from Renaissance through Gilded Age. It is not optional depth; the age-gate proofs demand it. Open the Vitae Compendium (the mod's own guide book) for detailed recipes; this entry maps where the ladder lives and why it matters.
Renaissance: Ara Vitae

Renaissance unlocks the Ara Vitae altar economy and the first blood orbs (Weak through Apprentice). The altar accepts LP donations from ritual sacrifice and self-sacrifice alike. Slates and runes are crafted here. The blood orb tier you carry determines how much LP you can hold and spend in a single ritual. The Vitae Compendium covers each tier's LP cap and how to advance.
Industrial Revolution to Gilded: Hellfire and Spiritus

In Industrial Revolution the Hellfire Forge opens. It consumes LP and raw materials to produce Hellforged parts, ingots, and Spiritus gems, the bridge between the altar economy and heavy industry. By Gilded Age, Hellforged material underpins Sentient gear, the NV capstone equipment. Sentient armor and weapons evolve based on kills: an investment that compounds across late-game play.
Realm Proofs
Access plus one proof per realm chapter.
What Each Realm Wants

Each realm chapter asks for access, bounded survival, and one grant-facing proof. Nether closes on a Blaze Burner after Mundabitur Dust and Blaze Powder work. End closes when you return to the Overworld after dragon and island work. Aether closes on the Slider kill. Eternal Starlight closes on the Gatekeeper kill. Undergarden and Otherside close on an Overworld return after their descent lanes. Archive closes on a Storage Tool; Observation closes on Blast Brick. Optional depth lives inside every chapter.
EndRem 12-Eye Route
Vanilla Eye of Ender is disabled. Portal needs twelve frames.
Twelve Frames, Ten Tracked Eyes

The vanilla Eye of Ender is disabled in the End Remastered config. The End portal still needs twelve EndRem eyes seated in its frames. The End Threshold quest batch tracks ten obtainable eyes: Magical, Nether, Corrupted, Cursed, Cold, Lost, Undead, Old, Black, and Rogue. Evil and Cryptic eyes are disabled in config and are not part of the route. KubeJS strips structure loot for the tracked eyes. Quest rewards hand out the first set. Renewable crafts replace eyes the portal consumes. Fill the remaining frames with other obtainable EndRem eyes such as Exotic, Witch, or Guardian. The End Threshold chapter teaches the full route.
Archive and Hand Sorting
AE2 and Refined Storage stay locked until IR.
How Records Work Here

Digital storage of any kind stays locked until IR. At Renaissance, sort with vanilla chests, label with books and maps, record findings in your Modonomicon, and use Better Archaeology's brush, artifact shards, unidentified artifacts, and archaeology table workflow for sample provenance. The Archive and Observation chapters run as parallel lanes. Depth over breadth.
Magic Foundations stations: Malum Spirit Altar with at least one Runewood Item Pedestal; Theurgy Calcination Oven and Liquefaction Cauldron; Occultism Golden Ritual Bowl, Dictionary of Spirits, and Book of Binding: Foliot; Neo Vitae Ara Vitae on the path to the Athanor. Spectrum starts in Spectrum Academy. Forbidden Arcanus Hephaestus Forge Tier 1 lives in the Nether Threshold chapter.

Mundabitur Dust requires Blaze Powder. The quest route points you at Nether Fortress blazes for rods and powder, then uses that work for the Blaze Burner proof. Mundabitur Dust is the F&A reagent step on the way there, and the same dust gates Deorum Ingot for downstream F&A. The Nether is required for Renaissance progression.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.loot.wha: Diamond tier and realm materials unlocked.

Open: diamond and vanilla late-tier gear, plus each realm's Renaissance-tier materials (Nether quartz, End basics, Aether bronze, Eternal Starlight samples, Undergarden Utherium, Otherside Resonarium). Soul Elytra becomes usable in this age but never drops from chests; craft or earn it through progression. Closed: Cursium Ingot from random or chest loot; it is the Maledictus boss proof. Closed: every IR and later tech material including Mekanism alloys, IE plates, and Oritech cores. EMI hides locked items.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.capstone: Vigil exam, capstone handoff, and IR unlock.

Maledictus Vigil is the Renaissance exam. Its readiness lanes cover equipment, damage, defense, and sustain, including Frostmaw (Mowzie's Mobs) and Nightmare Stalker (Born in Chaos). The wider age also runs Gatekeeper (Eternal Starlight) in its own chapter. Kill Maledictus (Cataclysm) for the Cursium Ingot. The kill alone does not grant Industrial Revolution. The Vigil capstone quest is the age handoff.

The capstone handoff is the Cursium Ingot quest in Maledictus Vigil. It waits until all ten chapter gates, Vigil readiness, and Maledictus are done, then accepts the ingot and grants Industrial Revolution. Rewards fire in order: AoA Maledictus journey advancement, Industrial Revolution stage, AoA Industrial Revolution age advancement, and Renaissance Seal. The optional Cursed Trophy in Magic Foundations is post-handoff cleanup for Apotheosis Frontier and is not required for Industrial Revolution. IR unlocks Immersive Engineering, Mekanism, AE2, Oritech, Modern Industrialization bronze, PneumaticCraft pressure, and deeper magic content (Theurgy Digestion Vat, F&A Aureal Tank, Occultism Djinni). See the Bosses category for Maledictus and the other Renaissance-gated bosses.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.bosses.d: Where to find the bosses that gate Renaissance and its realms.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.bosses.m: Cataclysm: cursed crusader of the Cursed Pyramid.

From L_Ender's Cataclysm. Maledictus is summoned inside the Cursed Pyramid, a surface structure that generates in desert biomes of the Overworld. Find the pyramid, reach its inner chamber, and perform the summon. It is stage-locked to Renaissance: an ancient curse repels your weapon until Renaissance is unlocked.

The Renaissance exam boss: a heavily armored crusader with mace slams, phantom halberds, bow volleys, and leap attacks. Bring diamond-tier gear, solid defense, and sustained damage. It drops the Cursium Ingot. Turn that ingot in at the Vigil capstone quest to grant Industrial Revolution. The boss kill alone does not grant the age.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.bosses.e: Cataclysm: defender of the outer realm.
Where to Find the Ender Guardian
From L_Ender's Cataclysm. The Ender Guardian is found in the Ruined Citadel, a structure in the End Highlands and Midlands (it avoids End cities). Stage-locked to Renaissance.
A shulker-like guardian with bullet barrages, teleport slams, and knockback waves. Use cover, keep moving, and punish its recovery windows with ranged damage. Drops End-tier Cataclysm loot.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.bosses.l: Bosses of Mass Destruction: cold-land lich.
Where to Find the Night Lich

From Bosses of Mass Destruction. The Night Lich is summoned at a Lich Tower, a surface structure that generates in cold Overworld biomes (snowy plains, ice spikes, frozen peaks, snowy beaches, frozen ocean). Climb the tower and use a Soul Star at the top to summon it; Soul Stars drop after killing enough mobs. Stage-locked to Renaissance.

An undead sorcerer with teleporting missile volleys and falling comets that can destroy blocks. Keep moving, dodge the comets, and close in when it teleports. Drops Bosses of Mass Destruction reagents.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.bosses.v: Bosses of Mass Destruction: roaming predatory bloom.
Where to Find the Void Blossom

From Bosses of Mass Destruction. The Void Blossom is spawned from a Void Blossom Boss Spawner, summoned with a Void Thorn. Its structure can generate across many Overworld biomes: oceans, rivers, beaches, mountains, hills, forests, jungles, taigas, plains, swamps, deserts, and caves. Stage-locked to Renaissance.
A burrowing plant boss that erupts spikes and slams down from above. Clear the arena, stay mobile, and read the burrow telegraphs. Drops Bosses of Mass Destruction reagents.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.bosses.u: Block Factory's Bosses: Nether arena knight.
Where to Find the Underworld Knight

From Block Factory's Bosses. Helvar waits in an Underworld Arena, an underground structure in the Nether (nether wastes and soul sand valleys). Find the arena door and open it with an Underworld Arena Key. Stage-locked to Renaissance.
A hellish knight with a thrown slash attack. Bring fire resistance and good armor, then punish its recovery after each slash. Drops Helvar's Sword and the Knight armor set.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.bosses.s: From Block Factory's Bosses. Sirok lurks in a Sandworm Nest, a surface structure in desert biomes of the Overworld. Stage-locked to Renaissance.
A burrowing worm with poison and lunge attacks. Stay mobile, watch for the surfacing telegraph, and punish when it surfaces. Drops the Sirok Gauntlet.

From The Aether. The Slider is the boss of the Bronze Dungeon, found in the Aether dimension. Enter the dungeon, reach its locked chamber, and strike the Slider to awaken it. Stage-locked to Renaissance.

A sliding stone boss that charges and bounces off the dungeon walls. Use the room's pillars to break its charge. The Slider only takes real damage from a pickaxe, so swing a strong pickaxe and keep swords off your hotbar for this fight. It drops the Bronze Key, which opens the dungeon's treasure room.

From Deeper Darker. The Stalker roams the Otherside, reached through an Otherside Portal deep below bedrock. It is a warden-tier elite, not a structure boss. Explore Otherside biomes and ancient temples to find one. Stage-locked to Renaissance.
A darkness predator that detects and hunts you by sound and sight. Sneak, manage light, and bring strong armor plus ranged options. Drops Otherside reagents.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.bosses.f: Where to Find the Forgotten Guardian
From The Undergarden. Forgotten Guardians stand inside Forgotten Vestige structures across the Undergarden dimension. Stage-locked to Renaissance.
A heavy construct that deflects frontal attacks. Flank it and overwhelm it between its slow swings. Drops Forgotten Nuggets, which forge into the Forgotten Ingot for upgrading Cloggrum gear.
book.aoa.dark_ages_manual.mechanics.cold: Sleeping bags do not warm you. They skip the cold.

Comforts gives you sleeping bags in every dye color. Sleeping in one passes time without resetting your spawn; that lets you skip dangerous cold nights early on. The bag itself is not a heat source. Cold Sweat keeps tracking your temperature the entire time you sleep, so a bag in the open snow will not save you. Pair the bag with a Cold Sweat heat block: a vanilla Campfire, a Boiler, or any block Cold Sweat reads as warm. The pattern is shelter, fire, bag, and only then sleep. Comforts plus a fire is a tactical retreat from a bad night, not a permanent fix for hypothermia.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.mechanics.create: Two workshops, running in parallel, not in sequence.

Overgeared anvils are operated by hand. Hold a Smithing Hammer and right-click the anvil yourself. Create cannot automate that step, and a Mechanical Arm will not forge tools for you. What Create can do is prepare the inputs. The Mechanical Press flattens ingots into plates and sheets. The Millstone crushes ores faster than a furnace. The Mixer handles bulk fluid blends. Run a small Create workshop beside your Smithing Anvil so materials are ready when you sit down to forge. Two crafts, one room.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.theme.age_of_the: Muscle, water, and wind before steam.

Medieval Times is the pack's first age of production. The Dark Ages taught you to survive. This age teaches you to build. Iron is open, Create kinetics are open, and the Smithing Anvil replaces the stone anvil as the heart of the workshop. Every machine in the age runs on muscle, water, or wind. There is no steam and no electricity here. Steam and early current arrive with the Renaissance, and full industry waits for the Industrial Revolution.

Use that as your placement tell. If a device burns fuel for power, holds pressure, or carries current, it belongs to a later age and stays locked. If it spins from a crank, a wheel, or a sail, it is yours.

Every other dimension stays sealed through this age. The Nether, the End, the Aether, Eternal Starlight, the Undergarden, and the Otherside all open at once when the Renaissance is granted. That is deliberate. Medieval Times wants your attention on the workshop: the mill, the foundry, the anvil, and the fields that feed you while you work.

The exit is not a portal. It is a hunt. When your iron kit is forged and your food line holds, take the What Waits in the Grove chapter and put down three of the four great beasts. Master of the Grove grants the Renaissance and unseals the six dimensions in the same stroke.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.mods.coke_and_ca: The coke oven and the foundry arrive together.

Two metalworking newcomers share the Metallurgy chapter. The first is Immersive Engineering's Coke Oven. Craft cokebricks, stack them three by three by three, and form the multiblock with the Engineer's Hammer. It bakes coal into Coal Coke and drips creosote as a byproduct, and creosote makes the treated wood that later engineering builds on.

The second is the Productive Metalworks foundry. Bake fire clay into fire bricks, assemble the foundry from its controller, tank, drain, and window blocks, and give it heat with a Liquid Heating Coil. Molten metal leaves through the Foundry Tap into the Casting Table or Casting Basin, where ingot, plate, and rod casts shape it. Follow the Metallurgy chapter quest by quest and you finish with a working metal shop.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.mods.alchemists_: Herbs, brews, and burrows in Relics and Burrows.

Alchemist's Garden opens with Medieval Times and runs through the Relics and Burrows chapter. The loop starts in the dirt. Craft Mixed Soil, pot it, and raise your first herbs. Grind harvests with the Mortar and Pestle to break them down into reagents, and craft the Alchemist's Glove so you can pick magical plants without setting off their effects.

The other half of the chapter is underground. Gnome Burrows hide gnomes, gnome gear, and stranger prizes, and the line runs on through Mushroomite tools, bezoar stones, and yeti antlers. Dig what you find and feed it back into the garden. The chapter walks the order node by node.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.mods.adventurers: Wizards, Paladins, Archers, Rogues, and spell craft unlock now.

A full rack of combat gear unseals with Medieval Times. Wizards brings wands, staves, and robes. Paladins brings paladin plate and the acolyte wand. Archers brings spears, the composite longbow, and archer armor. Rogues brings daggers, glaives, and double axes. Spell Engine sits underneath them, and its Spell Binding Table, spell books, and scrolls open now, along with rune stones and jewelry rings and necklaces.

No quest chapter walks these mods. Treat them as loot-and-forge depth. Hunt structures for pieces, craft what you can, and shape the kit to how you like to fight. The stage lock was the only thing in your way, and it is gone.
book.aoa.medieval_codex.mods.minecolonie: Found a colony. An ungated annex beside the age ladder.

MineColonies is not stage-locked and is not part of the age ladder. It lives in the Annexes chapter group with its own questline, so you can found a colony whenever you like. Medieval is the natural moment. Iron tools, steady food, and kinetic power make the early colony far less painful to feed and build.

Start with a Supply Camp on land or a Supply Ship on water, and take the Town Hall from the supplies inside. Place the Town Hall, recruit your first citizens, and raise a Builder's Hut so construction can begin. The Annexes questline then walks the working town: lumberjack, miner, farmer, fisherman, warehouse, and courier. A colony pays you back in food, materials, and a town that grows while you forge.
Forge problems, lock problems, and their answers.

Where is the iron smithing hammer? There is none. The Copper Smithing Hammer forges your iron work, and the next hammer tier is steel: the plain Smithing Hammer, forged from a steel hammer head.

My fired tool cast came back blank. You smelted it in a modded furnace. Iron Furnaces and their kin strip a fired cast back to a blank Clay Tool Cast. Fire and smelt casts only in a vanilla Furnace or Overgeared's Casting Furnace.

My forge rolls keep coming out Poorly. Quality is a roll, and the stone anvil caps it. Build the full Smithing Anvil by sneak-right-clicking a vanilla anvil with a Smithing Hammer, and reforge bad rolls at the Grindstone.

Why will diamond gear not craft? Raw diamonds drop at Medieval, but diamond tools, diamond armor, and diamond ore blocks stay locked until the Renaissance. Chest them for now.

Why can I not enter the Nether? Every other dimension is sealed until the Renaissance stage. The grant comes from Master of the Grove in the What Waits in the Grove chapter, after three of the four great beasts are down. There is no earlier door.

My Create machines stopped turning. The network is over its stress capacity. Add water wheels or windmill sails, or run fewer machines at once. The First Mill chapter covers the budget.

Something says it needs a later stage. Believe it. Locks in this pack are age gates, not bugs. The questbook order is the unlock order.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.mechanic: Apotheosis x Realm Loot
Affix gear rolls inside realm chests at age-appropriate rarity.
Where Affixes Come From

Apotheosis rolls rarity and affixes onto weapons and armor from chests, mob kills, and fishing. Once the realms open, Aether Bronze Dungeons, Undergarden Catacombs, Eternal Starlight structures, Otherside chests, and End caches become Renaissance-tier affix sources. Rolls are random, so do not save-scum chests. Reroll gear at the Apothic Enchanting Library or salvage what you do not need. Apothic Attributes governs how affixes hit your stats; Apothic Spawners stays gated for now. Mythic and ancient rolls pick up in IR. Renaissance is mostly common through epic.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.name: Gilded Ledger
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.tooltip: Scaled industry, the infinite grid, and the road to the atom.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.welcome.name: Welcome
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.welcome.descripti: What the Gilded Age is and what it demands.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.mechanics.name: Mechanics
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.mechanics.descrip: The systems this age runs on.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.recipes.name: Recipes and Seams
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.recipes.descripti: Where this pack rewires the stock recipes.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.capstone.name: Capstone
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.capstone.descript: The astral threshold and the atomic handoff.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.troubleshooting.d: Why some machines refuse to light up yet.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.welcome.where_you: The scaling age between iron and the atom.

The Industrial Revolution taught you steel, oil, and the first storage networks. The Gilded Age is where all of it scales. Refinery, infinite grid, heavy industry, oceanic industry, silicon, Chartered Arcana, and the astral boss route all feed the Atomic Disassembler handoff. Astral opens inside this age; atomic waits for the full proof matrix.

Use your tier-three Hephaestus Forge. That single act opens the Golden Workshop chapter and every Gilded proof lane beside it. The lanes open in parallel; explore them in any order, but the Atomic handoff still requires the major lane capstones.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.mechanics.golden_: Advanced table and auto bench at Gilded; elite and ultimate fusion tiers at Ascension.

The Advanced Auto Crafting Table is Extended Crafting's five-by-five automation bench, and it is what Gilded unlocks. Basic tables came from the iron era. The manual Advanced Crafting Table, along with the elite and ultimate fusion tiers, waits for Ascension.

The Ender Crafter works through recipes slowly, sped by ender alternators around it. The Flux Crafter does the same on Forge Energy through flux alternators. They are side benches for the age handoff, but the workshop chapter still tracks them and their auto variants. The Aether and Deep Aether quests in this chapter are optional mastery: gravitite, dungeon keys, skyjade, stratus, and keepsakes are useful side goals, not Atomic Disassembler prerequisites.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.mechanics.infinit: AE2 at scale, MEGA cells, and the RS aisle.

Your IR-era network grows teeth here: pattern providers and molecular assemblers craft inside the grid, wireless terminals carry it with you, and ExpandedAE multiplies the parallel jobs. Refined Storage runs the other aisle, with its own autocrafter and Mekanism chemical disks.

MEGA Cells raises storage cells from megabytes to the 64M tier here and scales crafting CPUs up to 256M. The 256M crafting storage appears here; 256M item, fluid, and chemical storage cells wait for Atomic. ExtendedAE's Crystal Assembler is its own little forge: most of that mod's devices can only be built inside it. The truly large assembler matrix is an Atomic concern.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.mechanics.data_ec: Industrial Foregoing and Hostile Neural Networks.

Industrial Foregoing handles the living economy: latex into machine frames, plant gatherers and mob crushers into resources, black hole units into bulk storage. Chemical Science splits crude into cuts alongside it. Do not skip the HDS Unit once fuel cuts matter; dirty diesel moves through pipes, but later recipes expect the scrubbed line. The refinery lane's stage proof signs off at the Laser Drill.

Hostile Neural Networks turns kills into data. A deep learner holding a data model learns from each kill; the simulation chamber spends prediction matrices to fight for you; the loot fabricator prints the drops. In this pack the Simulation Chamber also requires a NauTec aquatic chip, so the oceanic lab supplies part of the HNN brain.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.mechanics.oceanic: NauTec, lasers, and the biotech weaves.

NauTec runs on prismarine laser light. The deep sea drain feeds the lab, relays and the long distance laser carry beam power, and the augmentation station upgrades the player directly. NauTec is the proof spine: aquatic catalyst, chip, deep sea drain, and augmentation station close the ocean lane. Mekanism renewables and Electrodynamics machines support the same workshop; follow their quest labels for what is optional.

Three of this pack's weaves live here: the bio reactor requires EnderIO pulsating crystals, the mutator requires a PneumaticCraft vortex tube, and the simulation chamber requires NauTec parts. The bacterial mutator also needs containment shields before it will run. The mods are wired together on purpose; check the recipe before you assume the wiki is right.

Damaged aquatic chips come from NauTec's ocean ruins and machines. Treat ocean scouting as part of the lab setup: bring breathing, lighting, and inventory room, then turn salvaged chips into clean lab parts back at base.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.mechanics.charter: Chartered Arcana is no longer side flavor. Afrit binding, the Theurgy reformation array, Malum's spirit catalyzer, and Spectrum's spirit instiller converge into the magic authorization proof required by the Atomic Disassembler handoff. Those four checks are the required authorization chain; Mercury Catalyst and the Sun Chief trophy are local mastery, not extra Atomic Disassembler prerequisites.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.recipes.heavy_ind: Modern Industrialization and the iron giants.

Modern Industrialization climbs from steam to MV and HV here: hulls, coils, hatches, transformers. Immersive Engineering's arc furnace, excavator, and assembler join Immersive Petroleum's hydrotreater and coker on the same payroll.

Actually Additions closes the lane: the atomic reconstructor converts by laser, the empowerer charges items on four pedestals, and EnderIO's soul economy plugs in beside them. The heavy-industry stage proof signs off at the empowerer.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.recipes.silicon_c: Oritech wafers, the processing proof, and the Ascension table weave.

Oritech's electronics lane runs raw silicon into wafers and wafers into processing units. Crafting the processing unit is one of the Gilded proof checks. This pack rewires the Advanced Crafting Table recipe to seat a processing unit where stock would use a slate, so the Gilded silicon lane feeds the Ascension crafting bench. Machine Core 4, the assembler, and the Oritech reactor fleet grow beside that chain.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.capstone.power_be: The astral threshold and the Void Titan.

Astral opens inside Gilded, with astranite to refine, void gems to cut, and optional astral bosses you can save for later. The Atomic Disassembler handoff comes after that: it requires the five factory proofs, Chartered Arcana, the Void Titan, and the Qliphoth Malkuth exam boss. See the Bosses category for where to find each Gilded-gated boss.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.name: Bosses
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.descriptio: Where to find the bosses that gate the Gilded Age and Astral.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.ancient_re: Cataclysm: beast entombed in the Cursed Pyramid.
Where to Find the Ancient Remnant

From L_Ender's Cataclysm. The Ancient Remnant is awakened inside the Cursed Pyramid (a desert structure, Overworld) by excavating the suspicious sand within the pyramid. Brush the sand to rouse it. Stage-locked to the Gilded Age.
A dried-out titan that lashes and stomps; bring Gilded-tier gear and keep your distance during its slam. Drops Cataclysm remnant reagents.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.the_harbin: From L_Ender's Cataclysm. The Harbinger sleeps in the Ancient Factory, an underground structure that can generate in any underground Overworld biome. This is a Renaissance boss, not a Gilded one. You met it in the Renaissance Nether threshold line; it is listed here only for reference.

A multi-phase mech with lasers, charges, missile barrages, and a death-laser sequence. Hide behind cover during charges, break its stun window, and bring strong ranged damage. Drops Cataclysm harbinger reagents.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.ignis.name: Ignis
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.ignis.desc: Cataclysm: fiery champion of the Burning Arena.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.ignis.page: From L_Ender's Cataclysm. Ignis is summoned at the Burning Arena, a structure in the Nether wastes, using the Altar of Fire. This is a Renaissance boss, not a Gilded one. You cleared it back in the Nether threshold line; it is listed here only for reference.
An ignited champion with fireballs, abyss fireballs, and an armor/shield break sequence. Bring fire resistance and punch through its shield phases. Drops Cataclysm ignis reagents.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.void_titan: Where to Find the Void Titan

From Astral Dimension. The Void Titan is the Astral final boss, fought in a Final Boss Arena and released via a Void Titan Present block. Reach the Astral Dimension through an Astral portal (Golden or Amethyst Portal). Stage-locked to the Gilded Age.

The Gilded proof boss: its defeat (void_titan_defeated) is what the Atomic Disassembler handoff checks for. A heavy void boss with summon and projectile phases; bring your best Gilded kit. Drops the Void Titan trophy used for the Titan Helmet route.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.angel.name: Angel
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.angel.page: From Astral Dimension. The Angel is fought in an Astral arena; locate the Angel Debris / arena in the Astral Dimension and engage. Stage-locked to the Gilded Age.
A holy-flight boss with swooping attacks and projectile waves. Stay mobile and punish its descent. Drops Astral reagents.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.tower_of_m: Where to Find the Tower of Malice
From Astral Dimension. The Tower of Malice is engaged via a Tower of Malice Present block in the Astral Dimension. Stage-locked to the Gilded Age.
A vertical gauntlet-style boss. Clear the ascent and handle its spawning adds. Drops Astral reagents.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.helioos.pa: From Astral Dimension. Helioos is found in the Astral Dimension, recognizable by its hovering Helioos Ring. Stage-locked to the Gilded Age.
A radiant boss that grants vision and attacks through its ring. Close distance and burst between its ring pulses. Drops Astral reagents.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.undoing.pa: From Astral Dimension. The Undoing is an anomaly boss encountered in the Astral Dimension. Stage-locked to the Gilded Age.
A reality-warping boss with undoing anomalies. Stay grounded through the displacements and keep damage steady. Drops Astral reagents.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.amethyst_k: Where to Find the Amethyst Knight
From Astral Dimension. The Amethyst Knight guards the Amethyst Sanctuary structures in the Astral Dimension. Stage-locked to the Gilded Age as a boss-tier safety lock.
A heavy stepping knight with grumbling tells before its strikes. Bait the step, dodge, and punish. Drops Astral reagents.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.sun_spirit: Where to Find the Sun Spirit
From The Aether. The Sun Spirit is the boss of the Gold Dungeon, found in the Aether dimension. Stage-locked to the Gilded Age.
A fire-and-ice crystal boss that shoots Fire and Ice Crystals. Dodge the crystals and strike between its barrages. Drops the Gold Key, which opens the Gold Dungeon's legendary treasure room.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.eots.name: Eye of the Storm
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.eots.descr: Deep Aether: Brass Dungeon storm boss.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.eots.page1: Where to Find the Eye of the Storm
From Deep Aether. The Eye of the Storm is the boss of the Brass Dungeon, found in Deep Aether's sacred lands. Reach its chamber and hit the Storm to begin the fight. Stage-locked to the Gilded Age.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.eots.page2: A segmented wind boss that blows and shoots through its segments. Stay mobile, manage the wind pushes, and break the segments. Drops Deep Aether reagents.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.umvuthi.de: Mowzie's Mobs: sun chief of the Umvuthana Grove.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.umvuthi.pa: From Mowzie's Mobs. Umvuthi, the Sunbird, reigns over an Umvuthana Grove, a surface structure in savanna biomes of the Overworld. Reach the grove's throne. Stage-locked to the Gilded Age; his divine aura deflects you before then.
A radiant sun chief who summons healers as he loses health and offers the Sun's Blessing in trade for gold blocks. Suppress the healers and keep pressure on. Drops the Sol Visage and Umvuthana masks.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.capstone.power_be: The big Astral side branch is mastery, not the atomic key: trophies, presents, templates, necklaces, mobility gear, and full tool or armor sets are there for players who want the dimension complete. For the atomic handoff, follow the questbook through Astral access, astranite, the Void Titan kill, and the Titan Helmet. Void work and the final crystal support the local supreme altar route, but they are not listed on the Atomic Disassembler dependency fan-in.

The Void Titan is a required Gilded boss proof, and its Titan Helmet remains part of the atomic checklist. Atomic itself grants from the Atomic Disassembler quest after the remaining Gilded lane proofs are complete; the helmet alone does not unlock the next age.

Astral structures are worth mapping even when they are not the next gate. Towers, presents, smithing templates, and the final crystal all point to different parts of the dimension's loot web. When a quest names a template or crystal, treat it as part of that local Astral branch; the Atomic handoff still follows the required spine called out by the questbook.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.troubleshooting.d: Why Mekanism fission refuses to light.

Mekanism's fission reactor, its turbine, and the whole uranium chain stay sealed until the Atomic Age, no matter what the wiki says. The same is true of yellowcake and everything downstream of it. Gilded power is renewables, NauTec lasers, and MI turbines; the atom waits past the Void Titan.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.name: Atomic Dossier
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.tooltip: A field dossier for the Atomic Age of Ascension of Ages.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.welcome.name: Welcome
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.welcome.descript: What the Atomic Age is and how to survive your own success.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.welcome.where_yo: Atomic opens after the Atomic Disassembler handoff.

The Gilded Age ends when you finish the Atomic Disassembler quest. That quest grants the atomic stage. The Void Titan is a required Gilded proof on the way there, but the Titan kill alone does not open Atomic. This age is about energy itself: newly unlocked uranium and thorium sources, fuel pipelines that refine them, and reactors that scale from Create rotation to a fusion torus. The questbook walks the order; this dossier explains the ideas behind it.

Two rules keep you alive here. First, radiation is a real mechanic: it accumulates, it lingers, and it does not care about your armor bar. Build the hazmat rack before the fuel line. Second, the main fuel and reactor milestones are staged: the questbook walks you from Create-scale fuel into required reactor work, while optional reactor families and high-risk builds are marked as side depth. Build control and containment before you light anything.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.mechanics.name: Mechanics
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.mechanics.descri: The fuel economy, the reactors, the factories, and the war room.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.mechanics.nuclea: Uranium, hazmat, and the Create-tier reactor.

Atomic opens at Create scale: mine uranium from the ore sources this pack enables, crush it, cake it, enrich it, and load Create: Nuclear's reactor for rotation. Mekanism's full hazmat suit is the baseline. The Nuclear Science, reinforced, and Alex's Caves suit quests may log one piece, but their text tells you when a full set is the safety floor.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.mechanics.period: Chemistry, ore multiplication, and isotope infrastructure.

The Periodic Table chapter is the Atomic lab wing. It proves the Mekanism chemical chain, the Digital Miner, elite and ultimate circuits, storage, lasers, and cross-mod chemistry parts. The Digital Miner is filter-driven: set the target carefully and it becomes precise extraction instead of blind quarrying.

This lane feeds better fuel logistics, circuit substitutions, isotope work, and late storage, but it is not on the Chaos Gate checklist. The off-world isotope quest is a visible promise for the next age's rockets, not a blocker for Atomic. Build this lane when the reactor floor needs throughput or when the quest asks for a lab proof; finish dawn, chain, soul, war, prophets, and Geburah for the age handoff.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.mechanics.fuel_p: One barrel of yellowcake feeds the main fission families.

The main fission fuel chains in this age pass through yellowcake. Create: Nuclear presses its own. Nuclear Science leaches it in the Chemical Extractor. Mekanism enriches it from uranium ingots. Create: New Age's thorium route is its own side path. In this pack the yellowcake lines pour into one barrel: the Nuclear Boiler accepts Mekanism's yellowcake exactly as it accepts NS's own.

From the boiler the line runs gas to centrifuge to enriched pellets, and pellets press into fuel rods in three grades. Spent fuel is useful, not harmless: label and contain rods, dust, and salts until the reprocessor can handle them. Add scrubbers and sirens before the reactor room becomes routine.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.mechanics.three_: Create-scale nuclear work, fission, the MSR, and the fusion torus.

After Create-scale nuclear dawn, Mekanism's fission reactor becomes the first large FE reactor core: multiblock casing, fissile fuel, sodium loop, industrial turbine. Run it small until the logic adapter's alarms bore you. Nuclear Science's fission core arrives next, hotter and pickier, burning the rods your pipeline presses.

The Molten Salt Reactor is the operator's choice: its fuel is already liquid, so the failure mode is a frozen plug rather than a crater. The fusion torus is the summit. Thirteen by thirteen electromagnets, deuterium from water, tritium from sunlight, and megawatts that dwarf every earlier grid. For the age handoff, the checked proof is the Fuel Assembly capstone. NS fission, MSR, and fusion are reactor-depth milestones unless the quest dependency graph is tightened later.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.mechanics.machin: Factories in six tiers and storage with no bottom.

Machine Soul is the factory chapter. The gate-signing proof runs through Oritech core and forge work plus the Hostile Neural large simulation line. Stock Mekanism climbs advanced, elite, ultimate as core chapter work; the More Machines and Evolved addons add basic lines for new processes plus dense and overclocked tiers past the official ceiling.

The same chapter closes the storage arc: MEGA's 256M components and cells, ExtraDisks' infinite tier, Cable Tiers mega parts, the assembler matrix, and spatial IO that stores the room itself. If the gilded grid was a library, this one is a horizon.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.mechanics.thresh: The war chapter builds a deterrent, not a campaign. Required war proof is narrower than the arsenal: forcefield base, tier-one launch control, and the nuclear warhead proof. Higher silo tiers, payload galleries, turrets, lasers, and MI explosives are optional armory depth.

The MekaSuit is the age's personal endgame: powered plate, a module rack for work, mobility, and life support, and the Meka-Tool that replaces your hotbar's left half. Install radiation shielding before you walk a reactor floor in it, and the hazmat rack retires with honors.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.recipes.name: Reworked Recipes
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.recipes.descript: Where this pack rewires the stock progressions.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.recipes.yellowca: Mekanism's enrichment line feeds the Nuclear Science boiler.

Out of the box the two nuclear mods ignore each other: the Nuclear Boiler's tag only lists NS yellowcake. This pack joins Mekanism's yellowcake to that tag, so the enrichment line you built in the periodic-table chapter feeds the apex pipeline directly. If a boiler refuses Mekanism yellowcake, something has broken this bridge; report it rather than working around it.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.recipes.circuits: Elite and ultimate tiers print in three flavors; the quests accept each.

Elite and ultimate boards exist in three flavors here: Mekanism control circuits, Electrodynamics circuit tiers, and Chemical Science's organic boards from the spin coater. The questbook's circuit quests accept any flavor where mods genuinely overlap. Standardize on one line or run all three; the recipes do not care, only your logistics do.
Where the wikis and this pack disagree.
NS machines unlock here, not where the wiki says.

Public wikis assume you can build Nuclear Science machines the moment you find uranium. In this pack every NS reactor, the Mekanism fission multiblock, and the uranium economy itself are locked to the atomic stage. If a recipe shows in the viewer but refuses to craft, check the stage first: the locks come off in chapter order, not wiki order.

The three NS reactors share one fuel pipeline but not one difficulty. Fission needs a water pool and respect. The MSR needs its salts mixed in the fuel preprocessor first; a dry start does nothing. Fusion needs the complete electromagnet ring and BOTH cells, deuterium and tritium, loaded before ignition. If a remote plant behaves strangely after you leave, check chunkloading before rebuilding the reactor.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.capstone.name: Capstone
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.capstone.descrip: The four false prophets and the chaos convergence.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.capstone.chaos_c: Dawn, chain, soul, war, prophets, justice.

The age ends in Macabre's The Pit, an Atomic-locked dimension and boss surface. Valamon, Gomoria, Gargamaw, and Baal fall in order, each leaving a heart as proof. Bring the best kit you have earned from the war chapter. MekaSuit and modules help, but the gate checks the lane capstones, the four prophet hearts, Geburah's justice core, and the Alex's Caves boss ladder. After Geburah you fight Luxtructosaurus, then the Watcher, then Tremorzilla, and every kill in that ladder feeds the capstone. See the Bosses category for where to find each Atomic-gated boss.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.name: Bosses
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.descripti: Where to find the bosses that gate the Atomic Age.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.malkuth.d: FDBosses: Gilded Age exam boss, one of the three Qliphoth.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.malkuth.p:
From FDBosses. Malkuth is one of the three Qliphoth bosses. You already fought Chesed back in the Industrial Revolution; Geburah waits in the Atomic Age. Malkuth is the required Gilded exam boss.

His arena generates in deep ocean biomes in the Overworld. Locate the Malkuth Arena, then summon him inside it with an Eye of Malkuth. Craft or acquire the eye during the Gilded factory lines and carry it to the arena.

The mod's developers recommend fighting Malkuth at night, so start the duel after dark. Bring full Gilded-tier gear and be ready for a long fight before you place the eye.

Malkuth cannot be hit directly. His divine armor negates normal damage, so you fight him through the cannons built into the arena. You are made weak to Fire or Ice, shown on your crosshair, and you may only fire the cannon that matches your current weakness. Match the cannon to the weakness, fire, and repeat while dodging his slashes, boulder volleys, and impaling spikes.

He drops the Fire and Ice Core, plus the Malkuth Trophy and the Emberfrost Gauntlet. The core is the kill proof the quest checks for.

Clearing Malkuth grants malkuth_defeated. That proof is co-required into the Atomic Disassembler handoff, so this kill is part of what opens the Gilded to Atomic step alongside the Void Titan, the five factory lines, and the Chartered Arcana magic proof.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.chesed.de: FDBosses: Industrial Revolution exam boss, one of the three Qliphoth.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.chesed.pa: From FDBosses. Chesed is fought in the Chesed Arena, which generates in Overworld biomes. Summon it with the Eye of Chesed at the arena. Chesed is the Industrial Revolution exam boss, not an Atomic boss, so you cleared it two ages earlier. It appears here because the Chaos Gate turn-in accepts any of the three Qliphoth eyes: Chesed, Malkuth, or Geburah.
A merciful lord with roll attacks, robot armies, falling-block slams, and vertical electric rays. Clear the robots and dodge the blocks. Drops the Chesed Trophy.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.geburah.d: FDBosses: judge of the End arena (Atomic exam).
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.geburah.p: From FDBosses. Geburah is fought in the Geburah Arena, which generates in End biomes (alongside End cities). Summon it with the Eye of Geburah at the arena. Stage-locked to the Atomic Age. The kill grants geburah_defeated and the justice core. It does not grant Otherworldly. Present the justice core at the Chaos Gate capstone after the required Atomic lanes to open Otherworldly.

A judge of sins with judgement balls, ray and laser strikes, earthquakes, and the justice hammer. Do not let your sins pile up; keep moving and interrupt the hammers. Drops Geburah's justice core. Turn that core in at the Chaos Gate quest to grant Otherworldly.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.valamon.d: Macabre: first prophet of The Pit.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.valamon.p: From Macabre. Valamon is the first of the four prophets in The Pit, Macabre's Atomic-locked dimension. Activate the False Idol Valamon spawner to begin. Stage-locked to the Atomic Age.
A corpse butcher with bone-spike waves and a gut whip. Dodge the waves and punish between them. Drops the Heart of Valamon, one of the four prophet hearts the capstone checks.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.gomoria.d: Macabre: second prophet of The Pit.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.gomoria.p: From Macabre. Gomoria is the second prophet in The Pit, Macabre's Atomic-locked dimension. Activate the False Idol Gomoria spawner to begin. Stage-locked to the Atomic Age.
A fleshmonger monk that clones and grasps with mori hands. Handle the clones and break free of the hands. Drops the Heart of Gomoria.
Macabre: third prophet of The Pit.
From Macabre. Gargamaw is the third prophet in The Pit, Macabre's Atomic-locked dimension. Activate the False Idol Gargamaw spawner to begin. Stage-locked to the Atomic Age.
A grotesque consumer that spawns maggots and minions. Suppress the maggots and keep damage on the consumer. Drops the Heart of Gargamaw.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.baal.name: Baal, the Motionless Calamity
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.baal.desc: Macabre: final prophet of The Pit.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.baal.page: From Macabre. Baal is the final prophet in The Pit, Macabre's Atomic-locked dimension. Activate the False Idol Baal spawner to begin. Stage-locked to the Atomic Age.
A motionless calamity with whirlpools and malleus maleficarum finishers. Survive the whirlpools and land steady damage. Drops the Heart of Baal, the final prophet heart.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.morphegor: Macabre: fifth prophet of The Pit.
From Macabre. Morphegor is the fifth prophet in The Pit, added by the Dead God update. Wake the False Idol Morphegor spawner to begin. Stage-locked to the Atomic Age like the other four.

The tyrant splits into copies when wounded, so clear the splits before they surround you. Drops the Heart of Morphegor, the fifth prophet heart. All five hearts feed the Perturbation that summons the Dead God.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.infernal_: Block Factory's Bosses: Overworld dragon tower.
Where to Find the Infernal Dragon

From Block Factory's Bosses. Ashlord, the Infernal Dragon, is fought at a Dragon Tower, a surface structure in temperate Overworld biomes (plains, jungles, savanna, swamp, windswept forests, and the like). Stage-locked to the Atomic Age.
An infernal dragon with Ashlord Guards and flaming shooters. Clear the guards, manage the fire, and strike the dragon's windows. Drops Dragon Bones and the Ashlord Skull.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.capstone.chaos_c: Past the prophets, Geburah guards the justice core. The chaos gate accepts that core only after dawn, chain, soul, war, the silenced prophets, and Geburah itself are complete. The periodic-table lab lane is not on that checklist, but the Oritech Critical Assembly reactor is. Finish it, and the otherworldly stage opens, and the sky stops being a ceiling.

Most of Atomic Oritech is a fleet-scale hangar route, but one step is on the Chaos Gate checklist: the Critical Assembly quest, where you build the Oritech fission reactor. That reactor is required progression. The rest of the hangar route has its own capstone: cross-dimensional addon, magnetic field, plutonium, prometheum, tier-six processing, and the ultimate addon. Finish the whole hangar for stronger infrastructure; finish Critical Assembly, and the rest of the gate checklist, for Otherworldly.
advancement.aoa.journey.grove_trials.des: The first boss gate is behind you.
advancement.aoa.journey.maledictus.title: Maledictus Vigil Cleared
advancement.aoa.journey.obsidilith.title: Obsidilith Defeated
advancement.aoa.journey.void_titan.title: Void Titan Defeated
advancement.aoa.journey.leviathan.title: Leviathan Defeated
book.aoa.industrial_codex.name: Industrial Codex
book.aoa.industrial_codex.tooltip: Boss guide for the Industrial Revolution.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.welcome.name: Welcome
book.aoa.industrial_codex.welcome.descri: Steel, oil, and the first storage networks.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.welcome.where_: The Renaissance handed you the Cursium Ingot and the Industrial Revolution stage. This age is about steel, oil, and the first storage networks: Immersive Engineering, Mekanism, AE2, Oritech, Modern Industrialization bronze, and PneumaticCraft pressure. The questbook walks the order; this codex points you to the bosses you must fell along the way.

The bosses ahead are stage-locked to Industrial Revolution: the Nether Gauntlet in the Nether, the Netherite Monstrosity in the End's Ruined Citadel, the Obsidilith in the End, the Valkyrie Queen in the Aether's Silver Dungeon, and Skor the Yeti in snowy Overworld. Obsidilith grants obsidilith_defeated only. Gilded Age opens from the Iron Era Complete capstone after Chesed and the required IR lanes. See the Bosses category for where to find each.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.capstone.name: Capstone
book.aoa.industrial_codex.capstone.descr: IR spine, Obsidilith, Chesed, and the Gilded Age grant.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.capstone.leavi: Factory spine through Chesed, then the Gilded Age grant.

Walk the Industrial Revolution spine in order: Factory Discipline, Power and Motion, Pressure, Digital Logistics, then Magic Feedstock. Those lanes teach steel, grids, compressed air, storage networks, and magic feedstock into the factory loop. The questbook marks each lane capstone. Finish them before you treat the citadel as the age exit.

Obsidilith is the citadel exam. The kill grants obsidilith_defeated only. It does not grant Gilded Age. Fight Chesed next for chesed_defeated. The Iron Era Complete capstone waits on the required IR lane checks, Obsidilith, and Chesed, then grants Gilded Age. See the Bosses category for Obsidilith and the other IR-gated bosses.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.bosses.name: Bosses
book.aoa.industrial_codex.bosses.descrip: Where to find the bosses that gate the Industrial Revolution.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.bosses.netheri: Where to Find the Netherite Monstrosity

From L_Ender's Cataclysm. The Netherite Monstrosity waits inside a Soul Blacksmith, a forge that generates in the Nether across the crimson forests, warped forests, soul sand valleys, and nether wastes. Stage-locked to Industrial Revolution.
