The boss stands on the arena floor when the forge generates. If you find it empty, the Monstrosity was already killed or never spawned. Craft an Eye of Monstrous from four lava buckets, two netherite scrap, two blackstone, and an Eye of Ender, then use it on the Boss Respawner block to call the boss back. The fight itself: a molten golem that charges, slams the ground, and hurls debris. Bring Industrial-tier ranged damage and keep mobile. It drops the Monstrous Horn and scatters ancient debris on death.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.bosses.gauntle: Bosses of Mass Destruction: Nether arena fist.
Where to Find the Nether Gauntlet

From Bosses of Mass Destruction. The Nether Gauntlet is summoned in the Gauntlet Arena (a Nether structure) by using a Blazing Eye on the Ancient Carved Blackstone. Stage-locked to Industrial Revolution.
A spinning, laser-charging fist boss with energized and normal punches. Dodge the spin and the laser charge-up. Drops Bosses of Mass Destruction reagents and spawns ancient debris on death.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.bosses.obsidil: Bosses of Mass Destruction: End obsidian boss (Gilded proof).

From Bosses of Mass Destruction. The Obsidilith is summoned in the Obsidilith Arena (an End structure) by using an Obsidian Heart on the Obsidian Altar. Stage-locked to Industrial Revolution. The kill grants obsidilith_defeated. It does not grant Gilded Age by itself. Finish Chesed and the required IR lanes, then complete the Iron Era Complete capstone to open Gilded Age.

An obsidian boss with teleport bursts, anvil-drop attacks, and energy waves. Keep moving and punish the teleport recovery. Drops Bosses of Mass Destruction reagents and spawns an obsidian pillar on death. The Obsidian Heart is the kill proof the citadel line checks for.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.bosses.valkyri: Where to Find the Valkyrie Queen
From The Aether. The Valkyrie Queen is the boss of the Silver Dungeon, found in the Aether dimension. Challenge her in her arena. Stage-locked to Industrial Revolution.
A flying valkyrie who awards medals as you damage her and yields when beaten. Bring ranged options; the Valkyrie Lance helps. Drops the Silver Key to the Silver Dungeon's treasure.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.bosses.yeti.de: Block Factory's Bosses: snowy plains brawler.
book.aoa.industrial_codex.bosses.yeti.pa: From Block Factory's Bosses. Skor, the Yeti, lurks in a Yeti Hideout, a surface structure in snowy plains (Overworld). Stage-locked to Industrial Revolution.
A frost brawler with ice-slam leaps and a freeze wave. Stay mobile, dodge the leaps, and punish the recovery. Drops the Skor Gauntlet.
FDBosses: the required Industrial Revolution exam, first of the Qliphoth.

From FDBosses. Chesed is the required Industrial Revolution exam boss and the first of the three Qliphoth trials. Malkuth waits in the Gilded Age and Geburah in the Atomic. Craft the Eye of Chesed from four Sculk in a plus shape around an Eye of Ender in the crafting grid. Throw the eye and follow where it points, the same way you would track a stronghold, until you stand in the Chesed Arena out in the Overworld. Summon the boss there with the eye.

The kill grants chesed_defeated. It does not grant Gilded Age by itself. The Iron Era Complete capstone waits on the required IR lanes, Obsidilith, and Chesed together, then opens the Gilded Age.

Chesed cannot be hurt with a normal swing. During its Lightning Ray it summons Condensed Lightning crystals. Break one with an axe to become Energized, then stand at one of the arena's ray reflectors so the beam strikes the reflector. Each redirected beam deals one point of damage, and that is the only way through its guard.

Kill the monoliths as they appear. If any are still active when the finisher comes, the attack cannot be survived. Keep your eyes on the boss, because looking away invites its blinding gaze, and clear the robot adds between ray windows. Below half health it enters a second phase with faster, denser lightning.

The drop is the Lightning Core. Combine it with a weapon in a crafting table for a chance to call Chesed's lightning down on your targets.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.name: Otherworldly Codex
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.tooltip: Boss guide for the Otherworldly age.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.welcome.name: Welcome
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.welcome.desc: Abyssal depths and the Sunken City.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.welcome.wher: Atomic ends when you finish the Chaos Gate capstone and receive the Otherworldly stage. Otherworldly is the offworld age: Launch Window, Strange Dimension Ops, Dragon Technology, the Dyson Project, Digital Cosmos, then Beyond the Veil. The questbook walks that order. This codex covers the deep-ocean bosses that surface during the age, so use it as a boss companion beside the quest spine.

The Leviathan is this age's proof boss. Defeat it in the Sunken City for leviathan_defeated and Tidal Claws. The kill alone does not grant Ascension. Finish every required Otherworldly lane, then turn in Tidal Claws at the Beyond the Veil capstone to open Ascension. Scylla and Nerakyss the Kraken become fightable now too, but their kills are required later, as part of the Ascension finale before the Draconic Guardian. See the Bosses category for where to find each.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.capstone.des: OW spine, Leviathan proof, and the Ascension grant.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.capstone.lea: Offworld spine through Beyond the Veil, then Ascension.

Walk the Otherworldly spine in order: Launch Window, Strange Dimension Ops, Dragon Technology, the Dyson Project, Digital Cosmos, then Beyond the Veil. Those lanes teach rockets, offworld ops, dragon tech, megascale power, and late digital infrastructure. The questbook marks each lane capstone. Finish them before you treat the Leviathan turn-in as the age exit.

The Leviathan is the age proof boss. The kill grants leviathan_defeated and Tidal Claws. It does not grant Ascension. The Beyond the Veil capstone waits until every required Otherworldly lane is done, then accepts Tidal Claws and grants Ascension. See the Bosses category for the Leviathan and the other deep-ocean fights.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.bosses.name: Bosses
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.bosses.descr: Where to find the bosses that gate the Otherworldly age.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.bosses.the_l: Cataclysm: abyssal predator of the Sunken City.

From L_Ender's Cataclysm. The Leviathan is summoned in the Sunken City, a structure in deep ocean biomes (Overworld), using the Altar of Abyss. It is invincible outside of water, so fight it in its pool. Stage-locked to Otherworldly. The kill grants leviathan_defeated. It does not grant Ascension.

A multidimensional abyssal predator with tentacle strikes, bites, and roars. Stay in the water arena and dodge the tentacles. Drops Cataclysm leviathan reagents including Tidal Claws; its defeat also lets Coral Golems appear in the ocean. Turn Tidal Claws in at the Beyond the Veil capstone after every required Otherworldly lane to grant Ascension.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.bosses.scyll: Cataclysm: Storm Empress, required Ascension finale boss.

From L_Ender's Cataclysm. Scylla, the Storm Empress, is found in the Sunken City, a structure in deep ocean biomes (Overworld). She becomes fightable in the Otherworldly age, but her kill is a required Ascension finale fight. Scylla, the Kraken, and the Infernal Dragon must all fall before the final convergence at the Draconic Guardian opens.
A storm empress with howls and azure-seastone arena attacks. Weather the storm and keep pressure on. Drops Cataclysm scylla reagents.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.bosses.krake: Block Factory's Bosses: required Ascension finale boss.

From Block Factory's Bosses. Nerakyss, the Kraken, is summoned from a Kraken Spawner aboard a Kraken Ship, a surface structure in deep ocean biomes (Overworld). It becomes fightable in the Otherworldly age, but its kill is a required Ascension finale fight. The Kraken, Scylla, and the Infernal Dragon must all fall before the final convergence at the Draconic Guardian opens.
A tentacled abyssal boss with cannon and tentacle-smash attacks. Manage the tentacles and the cannon fire. Drops the Kraken Tooth and Kraken Trident.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.bosses.dead_: Macabre: the god the false prophets served.

From Macabre. Once all five prophets are dead, ring their hearts around four Abhorrent crystals to craft the Perturbation. Using it pulls you into The Pit and calls the Dead God. Gated to the Otherworldly age, so the hearts wait until you have crossed over.

The Dead God borrows the powers of all five prophets, cycling through their attacks. Read which prophet it wears and strike in the gaps. When it dies it leaves a Dead God Egg on the ground, and that egg is the door to Limbo.
book.aoa.otherworldly_codex.bosses.meshu: Macabre: the hidden god beyond Limbo.
From Macabre. The Dead God Egg cracks after three nights. Right click the cracked egg to fall into Limbo. Kill the Shadows there for Black Chalk, then press four pieces into a Tabula Rasa.

Use the Tabula Rasa on a living Shadow to summon Meshuggeneh, the hidden god behind the false five. It is the terminal boss of the Macabre road and the last required Otherworldly proof. The same Tabula Rasa also teleports you home.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.name: Ascension Dossier
book.aoa.ascension_codex.tooltip: Reactor discipline, chaotic crafting, and the final convergence.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.welcome.name: Welcome
book.aoa.ascension_codex.welcome.descrip: The final age: Draconic Evolution endgame.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.welcome.where_y: The Otherworldly depths are cleared and the Ascension stage is in your hands. This is the final age: Draconic Evolution's endgame. The questbook walks the order; this dossier points to the final boss.
The boss ahead is stage-locked to Ascension: the Chaos Guardian, Draconic Evolution's final boss, found on a Chaos Island in the End. See the Bosses category for where to find it.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.bosses.name: Bosses
book.aoa.ascension_codex.bosses.descript: Where to find the boss that gates Ascension.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.bosses.chaos_gu: Where to Find the Chaos Guardian

From Draconic Evolution. The Chaos Guardian is the Ascension final boss, found on a Chaos Island in the End. Awaken the Chaos Crystal to draw it out; Guardian Crystals heal it like the Ender Dragon, so destroy them first. Stage-locked to Ascension.

A draconic final boss with guardian lasers, Wither adds, and projectiles. Break the Guardian Crystals, handle the Wither adds, and bring your absolute best kit. Drops Chaos Shards and the Dragon Heart.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.luxtructo: Alex's Caves: walking-mountain apex of the Primordial Caves.

Luxtructosaurus lives inside the volcanoes that rise in the Primordial Caves, one of Alex's Caves' deep underground biomes. Find the biome first, then find a volcano in it. The beast sleeps in the volcano's core. To wake it you need an Ominous Catalyst, crafted from Heavy Bones and an Amber Curiosity you dig out of amber. Toss the Ominous Catalyst into the top of the volcano to trigger the eruption and raise the boss. Bring room to run and your best reactor-tier gear before you throw it. This is a required Atomic boss, stage-locked to the Atomic Age.

Luxtructosaurus is a walking mountain of an apex predator. It stomps, flings prey, and swings its tail, so keep distance and never get cornered against a wall. Chip it down while you stay mobile.

When it falls it drops Tectonic Shard in bulk. Collect the shards and turn them in at the quest to log the kill. This fight follows the Geburah exam and feeds forward into the Watcher, running Geburah into Luxtructosaurus into the Watcher into Tremorzilla, and the whole chain fans into the Atomic capstone.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.watcher.d: Alex's Caves: occult horror of the Abyssal Chasm.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.watcher.p:
The Watcher haunts the Abyssal Chasm, the flooded deep biome of Alex's Caves. It does not wander freely. Underzealots, the mole-like cultists down there, call it up through a Gloomoth Sacrifice to their dark god.

Go in deliberate and expect it to appear near that ritual. This is a required Atomic boss, stage-locked to the Atomic Age, and it comes right after the Luxtructosaurus kill.

The Watcher punishes carelessness and can possess its targets. Mind what it does when you look at it and control your approach rather than charging in. Deal with it on your terms.

Defeat it and take the Occult Gem, guaranteed on a real player kill. Hand the gem in at the quest. The Watcher feeds into Tremorzilla, which in turn drives the Atomic capstone, so it is the second-to-last link in the Atomic boss ladder.
book.aoa.atomic_dossier.bosses.tremorzil: Alex's Caves: the walking catastrophe that closes the Atomic Age.

Tremorzilla is not found. It is woken. You craft and hatch a Tremorzilla Egg using nuclear hellfire, then survive what climbs out. Keep the egg in mind as the trigger for the fight.

This is a required Atomic fight, not an optional deterrent. It is stage-locked to the Atomic Age and follows the Watcher in the boss ladder.

Tremorzilla is a walking catastrophe. It stomps, bites, swings its tail, and charges a radioactive beam that melts anything in its path. Break line of sight or move when it charges the beam, and never stand still. Your job is to survive and land steady damage.

Its kill feeds the Atomic capstone, the Chaos Gate at the end of the age, so the age does not close until Tremorzilla is down. It is the final link in the Geburah into Luxtructosaurus into the Watcher into Tremorzilla ladder.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.malkuth.de: Qliphoth boss: the required Gilded exam.
book.aoa.gilded_ledger.bosses.malkuth.pa:
From FDBosses. Malkuth is one of the three Qliphoth bosses. You already fought Chesed back in the Industrial Revolution; Geburah waits in the Atomic Age. Malkuth is the required Gilded exam boss.

His arena generates in deep ocean biomes in the Overworld. Locate the Malkuth Arena, then summon him inside it with an Eye of Malkuth. Craft or acquire the eye during the Gilded factory lines and carry it to the arena.

The mod's developers recommend fighting Malkuth at night, so start the duel after dark. Bring full Gilded-tier gear and be ready for a long fight before you place the eye.

Malkuth cannot be hit directly. His divine armor negates normal damage, so you fight him through the cannons built into the arena. You are made weak to Fire or Ice, shown on your crosshair, and you may only fire the cannon that matches your current weakness. Match the cannon to the weakness, fire, and repeat while dodging his slashes, boulder volleys, and impaling spikes.

He drops the Fire and Ice Core, plus the Malkuth Trophy and the Emberfrost Gauntlet. The core is the kill proof the quest checks for.

Clearing Malkuth grants malkuth_defeated. That proof is co-required into the Atomic Disassembler handoff, so this kill is part of what opens the Gilded to Atomic step alongside the Void Titan, the five factory lines, and the Chartered Arcana magic proof.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.theme.th: Why the Renaissance is the dimensions-and-magic turning point.

Earlier ages kept you on the overworld surface, mining down and building up. The Renaissance turns you outward. Six dimensions open here: the Nether, the End, the Aether, Eternal Starlight, the Undergarden, and the Otherside. Each one is a separate world with its own materials, its own hazards, and its own way in.

This is the design reason the Renaissance is called the threshold age. It is the first time progression stops being one place. You are no longer improving a single base. You are learning to reach, survive, and return from worlds that each want something different from you.

The magic mods activate in the same age the dimensions do. Magic Foundations requires Malum, Theurgy, Occultism, and the Neo Vitae Athanor close. Spectrum has its own chapter. Forbidden Arcanus and Mahou Tsukai open as further Renaissance depth.

That pairing is deliberate. Exploration hands you strange materials, and magic is the craft that turns them into power. A glowstone frame gets you to the Aether. Soulstone and spirits feed Malum's altar. Blood and life essence run Neo Vitae. The dimensions supply the reagents, and the magic mods consume them. Neither half stands alone.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.theme.de: Why the Renaissance runs ten required chapters and how to pace it.

The Renaissance runs ten required quest chapters, more than any other age in the pack. Magic Foundations, the Nether and End thresholds, Aether Literacy, Starlight Observation, the Undergarden descent, the Deeper Darker Otherside, the Archive, Observation and Experimentation, and the Maledictus Vigil all gate the age.

Each chapter records its own completion proof. The age does not advance until every one of those ten gates is done and Maledictus falls. There is no skipping a dimension you find inconvenient.

Density is the point, not an accident. Six dimensions and seven magic mods cannot be compressed into a short age without cutting content, so the Renaissance is built wide on purpose. Expect to spend real time here.

Work the chapters in parallel rather than one at a time. Many eyes, reagents, and materials drop as rewards from milestones in other chapters, so progress in one lane feeds another. Keep the required spine moving, take optional magic depth when you want it, and treat the age as a long stretch rather than a sprint.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.troubles: Each dimension needs its own portal or access item.

The Renaissance opens six dimensions, and each one is reached a different way. There is no universal portal. If you are stuck, you are probably using the wrong access method for that dimension.

The Nether uses the vanilla obsidian portal lit with flint and steel. The Aether is reached through a glowstone frame filled with a water bucket, not lit with fire. The islands float over open void, so bring blocks to bridge with.

Eternal Starlight is not built from scratch. You find half-collapsed Common Portal Ruins in the overworld and repair one to open the crossing. The Undergarden portal is a built frame opened by throwing a crafted Catalyst at it rather than using flint and steel.

The Otherside comes from Deeper Darker. Its portal is built from a frame inside an Ancient City in the deep dark, and activated with a Heart of the Deep recovered from that same city. Match the access item to the dimension and the crossing will open.
End Remastered replaces the eye portal with distinct earned eyes.

If you throw a vanilla Eye of Ender expecting it to point toward a stronghold, nothing happens. End Remastered changes how the End is reached. Vanilla eyes cannot be thrown in this pack.

Instead the mod fills the portal with distinct EndRem eyes that you earn across Renaissance. The portal frame in a stronghold stays dormant until twelve eyes are seated.

The End Threshold quest batch tracks ten obtainable eyes: Magical, Nether, Corrupted, Cursed, Cold, Lost, Undead, Old, Black, and Rogue. Undead is renewable by craft. Evil and Cryptic eyes are disabled in End Remastered config and cannot be obtained.

The portal still needs twelve frames. Fill the remaining frames with other obtainable EndRem eyes such as Exotic, Witch, or Guardian. Several tracked eyes arrive as quest rewards, so check your reward pile before hunting. Once you hold enough eyes for twelve frames, throw an End Remastered eye to locate a stronghold, then seat every eye in the frame to open the End.

The vanilla enchanting table works now that you have reached diamond tier and the Renaissance. Ring it with bookshelves for higher levels. Apotheosis later adds shelves that push past the fifteen-shelf vanilla cap, but the plain table is available to you immediately.

To close Magic Foundations, finish the Malum, Theurgy, and Occultism spines, then complete the Neo Vitae path to the Athanor. Start Neo Vitae with a Weak Blood Shard, craft the Weak Blood Orb, and place the Ara Vitae. Every Neo Vitae ritual and upgrade flows through that altar.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.mods.mag: Neo Vitae Athanor closes Magic Foundations enrollment.

Neo Vitae (neovitae) is the Magic Foundations close. Build the Ara Vitae blood altar path through Weak and Apprentice orbs, Blank Runes, and a Blood Shard reserve, then craft the Athanor. That Athanor quest grants ren_magic_foundations_complete.

Malum, Theurgy, and Occultism are required school spines in the same chapter. Their spine ends must finish before the Athanor unlocks. You cannot skip those schools and still close Foundations.

Once the Ara Vitae runs, place Blank Runes around it in the patterns shown in Neo Vitae's own guidebook to raise its tier. Higher tiers unlock stronger orbs and larger recipes.

The Athanor is the Foundations enrollment proof, not the forever pinnacle of all pack magic. Deeper Neo Vitae gear, the Hellfire Forge, and the Sentient armor line continue in later ages. Spectrum is taught in Spectrum Academy, not in Foundations.
Foundations schools plus Spectrum, F&A, and Mahou at Renaissance.

Magic Foundations requires three school spines plus the Neo Vitae Athanor close. Build each first station, then finish the chapter spine for that school.

Malum (malum): Spirit Altar, spirit harvest, and the Harvest of Souls end. Theurgy (theurgy): Calcination Oven, Liquefaction Cauldron, and the Sal Ammoniac Tank end. Occultism (occultism): Dictionary of Spirits, Golden Ritual Bowl, Foliot binding, and the Transporter Book of Calling end.

Spectrum (spectrum) has its own Renaissance chapter, Spectrum Academy. Start there with a Pigment Palette, Paintbrush, ink mixing, and Basic Pedestals. It is not a Magic Foundations starter school.

Forbidden Arcanus (forbidden_arcanus) and Mahou Tsukai (mahoutsukai) open as Renaissance age depth outside Foundations. F&A begins at the Hephaestus Forge and Arcane Crystal work in Nether Threshold. Mahou starts with the Mystic Code - First Sorcery and spell circles.

These mods run deep, and their higher tiers are staged to later ages. Do not expect them yet.

Occultism's Djinni summoning and its automation open at Industrial, with Marid summoning held back to Otherworldly. Theurgy's Digestion Vat and Forbidden Arcanus's Aureal Tank both unlock at Industrial. Finish Foundations enrollment now, then return for advanced apparatus once the next age begins.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.mods.dim: The Aether and Eternal Starlight open at the Renaissance.

Renaissance magic literacy opens travel to new dimensions. The Aether (aether) is a floating sky realm reached through a Glowstone frame filled with a water bucket.

Start with Skyroot tools and Holystone, then mine Zanite Ore for Zanite Gemstones and craft a Zanite Axe. Use that axe to harvest Golden Amber from Golden Oak Logs. A Bronze Dungeon houses the Slider boss. Defeat the Slider to earn the Bronze Key, then use it to open the reward vault. The Aether is a full toolkit tier, not a side trip.

Eternal Starlight (eternal_starlight) is a dim, dreamlike dimension of its own. Its portal and access are covered in the Starlight Observation quest chapter.

Inside you will find unique woods, the Starcore, and Aethersent Ingot, plus a roster of bosses like the Gatekeeper. Treat Starlight as an exploration and materials dimension that rewards careful navigation of its biomes.
The Undergarden and Deeper Darker Otherside open now.

The Undergarden (undergarden) is a lush underground world below the surface. Craft a Catalyst and use it to activate an Undergarden portal frame, the same way you would light a Nether portal.

Mine Cloggrum, Utheric Shard, and Forgotten Ingot, and watch for the Utheric Infection, a status effect that spreads if you let it. The Undergarden is a parallel resource dimension with its own metal tiers.

Deeper Darker (deeperdarker) extends the vanilla Deep Dark and opens the Otherside dimension below bedrock. Reinforce an Echo Shard into a Reinforced Echo Shard, the key material for reaching and surviving down there.

The Otherside is a sculk realm. Expect Warden Carapace, the Heart of the Deep, and Resonarium. The Deeper Darker Otherside quest chapter walks the descent step by step.
book.aoa.renaissance_compendium.mods.net: Vanilla Nether and End open, plus Apotheosis and End Remastered.

The vanilla Nether and End are gated to the Renaissance in this pack. They do not open in the Dark Ages. Forbidden Arcanus makes the Nether mandatory: Mundabitur Dust needs Blaze Powder, so the Nether Threshold chapter sends you to Nether Fortress blazes for rods and powder.

That same blaze work feeds the Blaze Burner proof and gates Deorum Ingot downstream. Plan a full Nether trip, not a quick dip.

End Remastered (endrem) replaces the vanilla End portal. The portal still needs twelve eyes seated in its frames. The End Threshold quest batch tracks ten obtainable eyes, including Undead, with renewable crafts for recovery. Evil and Cryptic eyes are disabled in config.

Fill the remaining frames with other obtainable EndRem eyes such as Exotic, Witch, or Guardian. The End Threshold chapter lists the tracked set and the recovery routes. This turns reaching the End into a long exploration goal without softlocking on a consumed eye.

Apotheosis (apotheosis) comes online as a support layer during the Renaissance and runs for the rest of the pack. It adds affix loot, gem sockets, and enchanting depth on top of your gear.

Collect Gem Dust and gem materials from tiered mobs, then use a Sigil of Socketing to add sockets and slot gems into weapons and armor. Apotheosis is not gated behind a station. It rewards you passively as you fight and loot, so read affixes on drops before you scrap them.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.mechanics.name: Mechanics
book.aoa.ascension_codex.mechanics.descr: The ultimate crafting surfaces of the final age.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.reactor.name: The Impossible Machine
book.aoa.ascension_codex.reactor.descrip: Build and run the required Draconic Reactor safely.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.capstone.name: Capstone
book.aoa.ascension_codex.capstone.descri: Chaotic crafting, the convergence, and Ascension complete.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.troubleshooting: When the final-age machines refuse to cooperate.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.mechanics.table: Extended Crafting fusion and the ultimate table.

Ascension runs on two crafting surfaces that dwarf anything from earlier ages. Extended Crafting supplies the tiered grid tables and the fusion system; Avaritia supplies the compression and Extreme Crafting path. You need both by the end of the age, so build the entry pieces of each before you chase the reactor.

Extended Crafting climbs Basic, Advanced, Elite, then Ultimate Crafting Table. The Advanced Crafting Table is stage-locked to Ascension in this pack, so do not expect it earlier. Each tier unlocks a larger grid and accepts the recipes the next tier of gear needs.

The second Extended Crafting system is the Crafting Core. Place a Crafting Core and surround it with Pedestals within a three block radius on the same Y level. The center item goes on the core and the surrounding items go on the pedestals. Feed the core power, because the recipe will not run without it.

The core is not instant. Right click the side of the core to see progress, stored power, and power cost. If a craft stalls, the core is usually short on power, not missing a recipe. The tiered tables above still handle most of the age's recipes, so use the Crafting Core for the specific combination recipes that call for it.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.mechanics.neutr: Avaritia compression and the Extreme Crafting Table.

Avaritia's spine is compression. The Neutronium Collector gathers cosmic material over time, and the Neutronium Compressor packs stacks of an input down into a single denser output. This is slow by design. Set the collector and compressor running early and let them work in the background while you build the reactor.

The compression path produces the singularities and the Crystal Matrix stock that the Extreme recipes consume. There is no shortcut that skips the wait.

The Extreme Crafting Table is Avaritia's nine-by-nine grid. It is where the Infinity Catalyst and the final Infinity path are assembled from compressed materials. It is a plain crafting surface with a huge grid, so it needs no power, only the right ingredients in the right slots.

The Ascension quest line points the Avaritia path at the Eternal Singularity and Infinity Catalyst. Feed the collectors, run the compressors, and keep the Extreme Table stocked so the capstone is not waiting on raw neutronium.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.reactor.require: Required win-gate multiblock. Build it correctly.

The Draconic Reactor from Draconic Evolution is not optional. It is a required Ascension win-gate multiblock, and the Impossible Machine chapter checks that you have built and run it. Treat this as mandatory progression, not a side project.

The core pieces are the Draconic Reactor Core, the Reactor Energy Injector, and the Reactor Stabilizer. The quest logs one of each, but the working multiblock needs four Reactor Stabilizers, one on each axis around the core, plus at least one Energy Injector to fuel it.

Place the Reactor Core with empty space all around it. Set four Reactor Stabilizers facing the core, spaced evenly on opposite sides, and link each one to the core. Add the Energy Injector on another face; this is where you pour power in to start the reaction and where you feed Awakened Draconium fuel.

Charge the core with energy first, then inject fuel, then bring the field online with more power. The reactor does nothing useful until the containment field is up and the temperature has climbed into its running band.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.reactor.reactor: Containment field, temperature, and the escape plan.

A Draconic Reactor is a controlled explosion. The Stabilizers project a containment field, and that field is powered by the energy you inject. If field strength drops toward zero while the core is hot, the reactor breaches and levels a large area. The single most important habit is to never let the field starve.

Watch temperature, field strength, saturation, and fuel conversion on the reactor interface. Keep field strength high and temperature inside its safe band. Rising temperature with a falling field is the failure spiral.

The reactor makes far more power than it consumes once it is running, but you must keep injecting power into the field the entire time. Build a large buffer before you light it. The Energy Core with Energy Core Stabilizers, driven through Energy Pylons and managed by the Draconic Energy Controller, is the intended sink and source. Route reactor output into that buffer and feed the field back out of it.

Always build a shutdown path. If the field cannot hold, stop injecting fuel, dump maximum power into the field, and let the core cool before it fails. Site the reactor away from your base and your Energy Core the first time you run one.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.capstone.dracon: Chaotic tier: chaos crafting past awakened gear.

The Draconic Heart chapter takes fusion crafting up to the Chaotic tier. Below it sits the awakened tier, built on the Draconic Fusion Crafting Injector and Draconic Cores. Chaotic gear is the top rung, and it demands Chaos Shards won from the Chaos Guardian in the End.

Chaotic recipes run through the Chaotic Fusion Crafting Injector and the Chaotic Core. These injectors draw even more power per craft than the awakened ones, so the reactor buffer you built pays off here directly.

Draconic Additions extends this tier with the Chaos Liquifier and the Chaos Infuser. Feed Chaos Shards into the Chaos Liquifier to build up Liquid Chaos, then use that stored chaos in the Chaos Infuser to turn a Dragon Heart into a Chaos Heart. The Chaos Heart is the key part for the top endgame gear and modules. This is where Chaos Shard drops become usable power rather than trophies.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.capstone.archiv: The Ultimate Block, the Infinity Ingot, and aoa_complete.

The final chapter converges everything. The Archive of Ages step assembles The Ultimate Block from Extended Crafting, the single most expensive compressed craft in the pack. Every earlier tier of table, catalyst, and component funnels into it. Completing it grants the archive proof and unlocks the last step.

Do not start this until both crafting spines are mature. The Ultimate Block wants the top outputs of the fusion system, and those want a running reactor for power.

The last craft turns The Ultimate Block into the Infinity Ingot on the Avaritia path. Completing that quest fires the final grants: it seals your run and awards the aoa_complete stage. That stage is the end of the eight-age climb.

Before this, the finale bosses must fall. Chaos Guardian, Scylla, the Kraken, and the Infernal Dragon feed the Draconic Guardian convergence that this age closes on. See the Bosses category for where each waits. Finish the bosses, finish both crafting spines, forge the Infinity Ingot, and Ascension is complete.
book.aoa.ascension_codex.troubleshooting: Fusion stalls, dead compressors, and reactor faults.

If a fusion craft never completes, the Crafting Core is underpowered. The Pedestals only hold items; all energy goes into the Core itself, so confirm the Core has a live power feed and let it fill before you start. If an Avaritia recipe refuses to craft, check that you are using the Extreme Crafting Table and that every neutronium input is compressed to the exact tier the recipe names.

If the collectors and compressors seem dead, they are usually just slow. Compression is deliberately gradual; leave them running and check back.

A reactor that will not start almost always has an incomplete multiblock. Confirm four Stabilizers are placed and linked and at least one Energy Injector faces the core. If field strength collapses the moment you inject fuel, your power feed into the field is too small; enlarge the buffer and the injection rate before trying again.

If a stage-locked table or block will not craft, the Ascension stage may not be granted yet. The Advanced Crafting Table and the final surfaces unlock with Ascension, so finish the questbook order rather than working around the lock.
